1.description: Greek GDP growth and CIPS GDP growth. Greek GDP growth (constant prices, seasonally adjusted) versus weighted-average GDP growth of CIPS (Cyprus, Italy, Portugal, Spain). Source: IMF.

2.description: Sample distribution of log(Assets) versus all Greek firms. This figure reports the distribution of log(Assets) for the 150 sample firms (red) and all 23,835 Greek firms in the 2014 Amadeus data (blue).

3.description: Histogram of markups. Histogram of the distribution of markups, defined as the difference between actual and break-even rates.

4.description: Actual rate and break-even rate. This figure presents the scatterplot of actual and break-even interest rates for new loans from 2015 to June 2017. The red dashed line corresponds to the 45∘ line. The blue solid line represents the OLS fitted line. The size of the circle corresponds to the relative size of the account. Black circles correspond to term loans, and brown circles correspond to credit lines.

5.description: Time-series of pass-through coefficients around the peak of the crisis. Time series of pass-through coefficients from Equation (19). Top Plot: Weekly pass-through coefficient (with confidence bands). Bottom Plot: Monthly pass-through coefficient (with confidence bands). The three vertical dashed lines in the two figures correspond to (a) May 1 (the expiration of the deadline to conclude the technical negotiations between Greece and its official creditors), (b) June 26 (the announcement of a snap referendum), and (c) July 13 (the surprising agreement on a third memorandum). We refer to the period between the first two dotted lines as the “deep crisis” period, and the period after the third dotted line as the “crisis abatement” period.

6.description: Alternative definitions of subsidization. This figure reports three alternative classification schemes for identifying subsidized loans. The green triangle points correspond to loans that receive a below-prime interest rate, defined as the minimum BE given to highest credit-rating firms in a month. The blue X-shaped points represent the loans for firms with interest coverage ratio below one for the previous 2 years. The red circle points are loans with interest rate below the BE (observations below the 45∘ line).

7.description: Actual rate changes by initial BE rate. These figures report the distribution of actual rate changes by initial BE rate. The sample is divided into three terciles, according to the initially reported BE rate (“low,” “medium,” “high”) of each corporate borrower in 2015. Each plot shows a histogram of the changes to a borrower’s actual rates from 2014 to 2015 for the corresponding tercile of initial BE rate. The black, vertical dashed lines represent the mean of each distribution, and the vertical, gray dashed lines represent the one-standard-deviation range around the mean.
